---
name: Obsoletion notice
about: This template is to notify ontology users of a term obsoletion.
title: 'Obsoletion notice: [GO ID: term label]'
labels: obsoletion
assignees: ''

---

Dear all, 

The proposal has been made to obsolete [GO ID: term label]. 
(keep one, remove others): 

* The reason for obsoletion is that the term is not clearly defined and usage has been inconsistent
*  The reason for obsoletion is that the meaning of the term is ambiguous.
*  The reason for obsoletion is that this term represents a GO-CAM model.
*  The reason for obsoletion is that this term represent an assay and not a real GO process. 
* The reason for obsoletion is that this represents a phenotype.
*  The reason for obsoletion is that the data from the paper for which the term was requested can be accurately described using [appropriate GO term] 
* The reason for obsoletion is that this term represents a gene product.
* The reason for obsoletion is that this term refers to a class of proteins.
*  The reason for obsoletion is that this term was added in error.
* The reason for obsoletion is that more specific terms were created.
*  The reason for obsoletion is that this term was an unnecessary grouping term.
* The reason for obsoletion is that is not known to be catalyzed by any gene product.
*  The reason for obsoletion is that it more specific than the specificity of any known gene product.
* The reason for obsoletion is that there is no evidence that this function/process/component exists.
* (or add other reason)

Annotations:
There are X experimental annotations to this term by (list of impacted groups and their respective numbers of EXP and ISS annotations). 
or: There are no experimental annotations to this term.

There are X InterPro2GO/UniRule/UniProtKW mappings to this term. 
or: There are no mappings to this term.

This term is included in the XXX subset(s).
or: This term is not present in any subset.

Annotations can be viewed here: [link to GitHub ticket in the go-annotation tracker].

If obsolete term has a 'replaced by' term: (optional)
This term is being replaced by [GO term] 

If obsolete term has a 'consider' term:  (optional)
Annotations can be transferred to [suggested GO term(s)] 

Any comments can be  added to the issue: [link to GitHub ticket in the go-ontology tracker]. 
Unless objections are received by [DATE], we will assume that you agree to this change.

Thanks, 

The GO-Ontology Editors

{"body":"## Summary

Per the obsoletion notice geneontology/go-announcements#1152, obsoletes the biological_process terms that represent a molecular function. The new MF term GO:0160321 vesicle docking activity (created in this same issue) is the primary target for re-annotation, along with GO:7770062 vesicle membrane tethering activity.

Refs #31880.

## Terms obsoleted (per the announcement)

| GO ID | Label |
|-------|-------|
| GO:0048278 | vesicle docking |
| GO:0048211 | Golgi vesicle docking |
| GO:0090384 | phagosome-lysosome docking |
| GO:0106020 | regulation of vesicle docking |
| GO:0106022 | positive regulation of vesicle docking |
| GO:0006904 | vesicle docking involved in exocytosis |
| GO:0106021 | negative regulation of vesicle docking |
| GO:0016081 | synaptic vesicle docking |
| GO:0061790 | dense core granule docking |

Each was rewritten to: name prefixed with `obsolete `, def prefixed with `OBSOLETE.`, comment giving the obsoletion reason, `term_tracker_item` set to this issue, `is_obsolete: true`, and `consider:` lines pointing to GO:0160321 (and where applicable GO:7770062). All `is_a`, `relationship`, `intersection_of`, `synonym`, and `subset` tags were stripped.

## Additional term obsoleted (not in the announcement - please review)

- **GO:0099148 regulation of synaptic vesicle docking** - its logical definition (`intersection_of: regulates GO:0016081`) was rendered invalid by the obsoletion of GO:0016081. It also follows the same rationale (its target is now a MF). I obsoleted it consistently with the others, but it was not listed in #1152 - flagging for review in case the GO-Ontology Editors want to amend the announcement / annotation review.

## Rewires for dangling references

- **GO:0001927 exocyst assembly** - removed `relationship: part_of GO:0006904`
- **GO:0001928 regulation of exocyst assembly** - removed `relationship: regulates GO:0048278`
- **GO:0048277 obsolete nonexocytotic vesicle docking** - `replaced_by: GO:0048278` -> `consider: GO:0160321` (replaced_by target was being obsoleted)
- **GO:0090388 obsolete phagosome-lysosome docking involved in apoptotic cell clearance** - `consider: GO:0090384` swapped for `consider: GO:0160321` (kept the other consider, GO:0090387)

## Note on consider terms

The announcement says \"Annotations can be transferred to GO:0160321 vesicle docking activity or GO:0099023 vesicle tethering activity.\" GO:0099023 is actually `vesicle tethering complex` (a cellular_component). The corresponding MF is GO:7770062 `vesicle membrane tethering activity` (which has `vesicle tethering activity` as an EXACT synonym). I used GO:7770062 in the `consider:` lines.

## Test plan

- [x] PRE-VALIDATION: `make travis_build` passed before changes
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION: `make travis_build` passes after changes (all SPARQL violation rules PASS, reasoner OK)
- [x] No dangling axiom references remain to the obsoleted IDs
- [x] All obsolete terms h

input.command:
cat > /tmp/comment.md << 'EOF'
@raymond91125 Draft announcement email below, built from the [obsoletion-notice template](https://github.com/geneontology/go-announcements/blob/master/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/obsoletion-notice.md) with all instructional/alternative lines stripped. It now covers all 10 terms (the 9 from geneontology/go-announcements#1152 plus GO:0099148, which you added later).

**Suggested issue title:** `Obsoletion notice: GO:0048278 vesicle docking, descendants and regulation terms`

---

Dear all,

The proposal has been made to obsolete the following terms:

- GO:0048278 vesicle docking
- GO:0048211 Golgi vesicle docking
- GO:0090384 phagosome-lysosome docking
- GO:0106020 regulation of vesicle docking
- GO:0106022 positive regulation of vesicle docking
- GO:0006904 vesicle docking involved in exocytosis
- GO:0106021 negative regulation of vesicle docking
- GO:0016081 synaptic vesicle docking
- GO:0061790 dense core granule docking
- GO:0099148 regulation of synaptic vesicle docking

The reason for obsoletion is that these terms represent a molecular function.

There are experimental annotations to these terms by the following groups: BHF-UCL (5), ComplexPortal (52), HGNC-UCL (1), MGI (7), PINC (4), ParkinsonsUK-UCL (4), RGD (7), SGD (15), UniProt (12), dictyBase (1), FlyBase (8), SynGO (10), WB (1), DisProt (1), SynGO-UCL (2).

There are no InterPro2GO/UniRule/UniProtKW mappings to these terms.

GO:0016081 and GO:0099148 are included in the goslim_synapse subset.

Annotations can be viewed here: https://github.com/geneontology/go-annotation/issues/6379

Annotations can be transferred to GO:0160321 vesicle docking activity or GO:7770062 vesicle membrane tethering activity. For GO:0099148 regulation of synaptic vesicle docking, GO:0016082 synaptic vesicle priming or a regulation of exocytosis term may be more appropriate depending on the gene product.

Any comments can be added to the issue: https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31880
Unless objections are received by 2026-05-29, we will assume that you agree to this change.

Thanks,

The GO-Ontology Editors

---

A few notes on the draft (not part of the email):

- **Annotation counts**: the per-group breakdown is taken verbatim from the annotation review ticket (geneontology/go-annotation#6379), which was scoped to the original 9 terms. GO:0099148 was added to the obsoletion set afterwards; its experimental annotations (the tom1-1 / camk2a / septin cases discussed above) are not reflected in those numbers. If you want a precise total I can pull updated per-group counts, otherwise the editors may want to fold GO:0099148 into the same review.
- **Transfer target correction**: the original notice (#1152) and go-annotation#6379 listed "GO:0099023 vesicle tethering activity", but GO:0099023 is `vesicle tethering complex` (a cellular_component). The correct MF is GO:7770062 `vesicle membrane tethering activity`. I've used the corrected ID in the draft, consistent with the fix @hattrill flagged and you confirmed.
- **Comment deadline** is set to two weeks out (2026-05-29) — adjust as you see fit.
- The terms are not yet obsoleted on master; PR #32073 (the obsoletion edits, incl. GO:0099148) is still open.

---

- Model: `claude-opus-4-7`
- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @raymond91125
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/25942352106)

content.text:
Posted the drafted announcement on issue #31880.

Summary of what I did:

- Fetched the only template in `geneontology/go-announcements/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE` (`obsoletion-notice.md`) and filled it in with real data, removing all the template's instructional lines, unused reason options, and `or:`/conditional alternatives.
- Scoped the notice to all **10 terms** — the 9 in go-announcements#1152 plus **GO:0099148 regulation of synaptic vesicle docking**, which @raymond91125 added later in this thread.
- Pulled the impacted-group annotation breakdown from the annotation review ticket (go-annotation#6379), recorded "no InterPro2GO/UniRule/UniProtKW mappings", and noted that GO:0016081 and GO:0099148 are in `goslim_synapse`.
- Corrected the transfer target: the old notice listed GO:0099023 ("vesicle tethering activity"), but that ID is actually `vesicle tethering complex` (CC). The draft uses GO:7770062 `vesicle membrane tethering activity` alongside GO:0160321, matching the fix @hattrill flagged.

Flagged three things for the editors: the per-group counts predate GO:0099148's addition (its annotations aren't included in those numbers), the transfer-ID correction, and that the obsoletion PR #32073 is still open so the terms aren't yet obsolete on master. The comment deadline is a placeholder (2026-05-29) for them to adjust.

No ontology files were changed and no PR was needed — this was a drafting/communication task only.
